meta.title: Our Recipe Library
meta.subtitle: Every recipe we’ve built. All gluten-free. All adaptable to Meg’s low glycemic needs.
meta.sweetenerNote: Wherever a recipe below calls for honey, maple syrup, or allulose, substitute monk fruit 1:1 in its place. This applies across the entire library going forward.

recipes.name: Thai Peanut Chicken Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Ground chicken in a sesame-peanut sauce, served in butter lettuce cups. Tested and confirmed household favorite.
recipes.tagline: Quick, low-carb, and gluten-free. A confirmed household favorite.

instructions.title: Make the peanut sauce
instructions.body: Whisk together peanut butter, tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, lime juice, and monk fruit sweetener. Add sriracha if using.

instructions.title: Cook the chicken
instructions.body: Heat avocado oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds. Add chicken, cook until no pink remains.

instructions.title: Add the sauce
instructions.body: Pour the sauce over the chicken and stir to coat. Cook 1-2 minutes until absorbed and slightly caramelized.

instructions.title: Assemble and serve
instructions.body: Spoon into lettuce leaves. Top with carrots, green onions, cilantro, and crushed peanuts. Serve with lime wedges.

recipes.notes: Butter lettuce works best as cups. Use tamari or coconut aminos, never standard soy sauce, to keep this GF.

recipes.name: Korean Beef Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Garlicky ground beef glazed in a sesame-ginger sauce. GF gochujang required.
recipes.tagline: Garlicky ground beef glazed in a sesame-ginger sauce, served in crisp lettuce cups.

instructions.title: Mix the sauce
instructions.body: Whisk tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, gochujang, monk fruit sweetener, and black pepper.

instructions.title: Brown the beef
instructions.body: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and ginger, cook 30 seconds. Add beef, cook until browned through, draining excess fat if needed.

instructions.title: Add sauce and finish
instructions.body: Pour sauce over the beef, stir to coat, cook 2 minutes until glazed. Stir in green onions and sesame seeds.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.body: Spoon into lettuce cups and serve with toppings of choice.

recipes.notes: Standard gochujang contains wheat. Look for Bibigo GF Gochujang or Mother-in-Law's brand specifically.

recipes.name: Mexican Taco Lettuce Wraps
recipes.description: Ground beef or chicken with a homemade GF taco seasoning blend, served in lettuce cups with a full topping bar.
recipes.tagline: All the flavor of taco night in a crisp lettuce cup. Works with chicken or beef.

instructions.title: Season the meat
instructions.body: Toss the ground meat with the full spice blend until evenly coated.

instructions.title: Cook
instructions.body: Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic, cook 30 seconds. Add seasoned meat, cook until done, breaking it apart.

instructions.title: Add liquid and finish
instructions.body: Add broth and lime juice. Cook 2 minutes until absorbed and well glazed.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.body: Spoon into lettuce cups and load with toppings.

recipes.notes: Skip packet taco seasoning, most contain gluten fillers. This homemade blend is fully GF.

recipes.name: Thai Peanut Pulled Pork (Instant Pot)
recipes.description: Pork shoulder braised in peanut-ginger-lime sauce in the Instant Pot. Pressure cook 60 minutes, shred, finish with lime.
recipes.tagline: Tender shredded pork shoulder in a bold sesame-peanut sauce. Great in lettuce wraps, over rice, or on its own.

ingredients.item: pork shoulder or butt, cut into chunks

instructions.title: Season and sear
instructions.body: Season pork with salt and pepper. Sear in batches in the Instant Pot on Sauté mode, 2-3 min per side. Remove and set aside.

instructions.title: Make the sauce
instructions.body: Whisk peanut butter, tamari, sesame oil, rice vinegar, monk fruit, garlic, ginger, sriracha, and fish sauce until smooth.

instructions.title: Pressure cook
instructions.body: Add broth to deglaze the pot. Return pork, pour sauce over top. Seal and cook on High Pressure 60 minutes.

instructions.title: Natural release
instructions.body: Let pressure release naturally 15 minutes, then vent any remaining steam.

instructions.title: Shred and finish
instructions.body: Shred pork with two forks. Skim excess fat, stir in lime juice, return pork to sauce.

instructions.title: Reduce if needed
instructions.body: Simmer uncovered on Sauté mode 5-8 minutes if sauce needs to thicken.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.body: Pile into lettuce cups or over rice. Top with green onions, cilantro, and crushed peanuts.

recipes.notes: Use pork shoulder or butt only, not loin which will dry out. Fish sauce adds essential depth, don't skip it.

recipes.name: Instant Pot Teriyaki Chuck Roast
recipes.description: Boneless chuck roast seasoned with Spiceology Teriyaki Takeout, seared, then pressure cooked until tender.
recipes.tagline: Simple, bold, and tender. The Spiceology Teriyaki Takeout blend does all the heavy lifting.

instructions.title: Season the roast
instructions.body: Pat roast dry, coat all sides generously with seasoning, pressing it in.

instructions.title: Sear
instructions.body: Heat oil in Instant Pot on Sauté mode. Sear roast 3-4 min per side until deep brown crust forms. Remove.

instructions.title: Deglaze
instructions.body: Pour in broth, scrape up browned bits. Add garlic. Return roast to pot.

instructions.title: Pressure cook
instructions.body: Seal and cook on High Pressure 75 minutes for a 3 lb roast, adding 10 minutes per additional pound.

instructions.title: Natural release
instructions.body: Let pressure release naturally at least 20 minutes before venting.

instructions.title: Rest and serve
instructions.body: Rest 5-10 minutes before slicing. Spoon pan juices over the top.

recipes.notes: Chuck roast is the right cut, leaner cuts will turn out dry. For pulled texture, add 15-20 minutes to the cook time.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Shrimp over Arugula Salad
recipes.description: Seared shrimp finished in garlic butter and chili flakes over a lemon parmesan arugula salad. Ready in 15 minutes.
recipes.tagline: Garlicky, slightly spicy shrimp over a bright lemony arugula salad. Done in 15 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, dijon, monk fruit sweetener, and salt. Set aside.

instructions.title: Prep the shrimp
instructions.body: Pat shrimp very dry. Season with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Sear the shrimp
instructions.body: Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook shrimp 90 seconds without moving, flip, cook 60 seconds more.

instructions.title: Add garlic butter
instructions.body: Reduce heat, add butter, garlic, and chili flakes. Toss shrimp 60 seconds. Squeeze lemon juice over and remove from heat.

instructions.title: Assemble and serve
instructions.body: Toss arugula and tomatoes with dressing, top with parmesan, pile shrimp on top.

recipes.notes: Pat shrimp very dry before cooking. Dress the arugula just before serving, it wilts quickly.

recipes.name: Chicken and Zucchini Cabbage Rolls
recipes.description: Ground chicken and finely diced zucchini wrapped in napa cabbage, pan-fried, served with a sesame dipping sauce. Zucchini stands in for mushrooms.
recipes.tagline: Savory ground chicken and zucchini wrapped in tender napa cabbage, pan-fried golden, served with a sesame dipping sauce.

instructions.title: Cook the zucchini
instructions.body: Heat oil in a skillet, add zucchini with a pinch of salt, cook until moisture releases and it begins to brown, about 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Build the filling
instructions.body: Add white parts of green onion and garlic, cook 1 minute. Season with pepper, ginger powder, soy sauce. Add chicken, cook until done.

instructions.title: Prepare the cabbage
instructions.body: Boil cabbage leaves 5-10 minutes until tender and pliable. Pat very dry.

instructions.title: Make the sauce
instructions.body: Whisk soy sauce, monk fruit sweetener, rice vinegar, sesame oil, garlic powder, and green onion tops.

instructions.title: Assemble the rolls
instructions.body: Place filling near the base of each leaf, fold sides in, roll up firmly.

instructions.title: Pan-fry
instructions.body: Brown rolls seam-side down first, then all sides, about 6-8 minutes total.

instructions.title: Sauce and serve
instructions.body: Serve hot with sauce poured over, garnished with sesame seeds and green onion.

recipes.notes: Zucchini replaces mushrooms here for those who don't like them, doing the same job of releasing moisture into the filling.

recipes.name: Thai-Style Steak Salad
recipes.description: Seared flat iron or flank steak sliced thin over greens with mint, cilantro, and a 3:1 lime-to-fish-sauce dressing. Dress within 5 minutes of serving.
recipes.tagline: Thin-sliced seared steak over a bright Thai-inspired salad with fresh herbs, crunch, and a punchy lime dressing.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk lime juice, fish sauce, monk fruit sweetener, garlic, chili flakes, and sesame oil until combined.

instructions.title: Prep the steak
instructions.body: Pat steak dry, season both sides with salt and pepper.

instructions.title: Sear the steak
instructions.body: Heat oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat until smoking. Sear 4-5 min per side for medium-rare.

instructions.title: Rest
instructions.body: Rest on a cutting board for 10 minutes. Do not skip this.

instructions.title: Build the salad
instructions.body: While resting, chop herbs and combine with greens, carrots, cucumber, and green onions. Hold off on dressing until just before plating.

instructions.title: Dress, slice, and serve
instructions.body: Dress gradually, tossing and stopping when lightly coated. Slice steak thin against the grain, arrange over salad, finish with peanuts.

recipes.notes: Flat iron needs a longer sear than flank. Slice against the grain always. Don't dress more than 5 minutes before serving.

recipes.name: Mixed Greens Salad with Prosciutto, Mozzarella and Peach
recipes.description: Mixed greens (no arugula or spinach), prosciutto, fresh mozzarella, peach as an accent, toasted pine nuts, lemon vinaigrette.
recipes.tagline: A bright, fresh salad with salty prosciutto, creamy fresh mozzarella, toasted pine nuts, and peach as a subtle accent.

instructions.title: Toast the pine nuts
instructions.body: Toast in a dry skillet over medium heat 2-3 minutes until golden, stirring frequently. Remove immediately.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, dijon, salt, and pepper until emulsified.

instructions.title: Build the salad
instructions.body: Arrange greens on a platter. Tuck prosciutto, mozzarella, and peach throughout. Scatter red onion and pine nuts.

instructions.title: Dress and serve
instructions.body: Drizzle dressing gradually just before serving, tossing lightly.

recipes.notes: Half a peach keeps the fruit as an accent, not the focal point. Check bagged greens for arugula or spinach before buying.

recipes.name: Mango Slaw
recipes.description: Shredded cabbage, mango, carrots, herbs, and lime-honey dressing. Rest 10 minutes before serving for best texture.
recipes.tagline: Cool, bright, and slightly sweet. A natural partner for Thai peanut chicken wraps and grilled pork.

ingredients.item: ripe mango, julienned or diced small

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk lime juice, rice vinegar, sesame oil, monk fruit sweetener, and salt until dissolved.

instructions.title: Combine the slaw
instructions.body: Add cabbage, mango, carrots, green onions, and cilantro to a large bowl. Add jalapeño and red pepper flakes if using.

instructions.title: Dress and toss
instructions.body: Pour dressing over slaw and toss well to coat.

instructions.title: Rest before serving
instructions.body: Let sit at least 10 minutes before serving so the cabbage softens slightly.

recipes.notes: Use a ripe but firm mango so it holds its shape. Keeps in the fridge up to 2 days.

recipes.name: Cucumber Sesame Salad
recipes.description: Thin-sliced cucumber, rice vinegar, sesame oil, green onion, sesame seeds. Ready in 10 minutes, no heat required.
recipes.tagline: Cool, crisp, and ready in 10 minutes. No heat required.

instructions.title: Prep the cucumbers
instructions.body: Slice as thin as possible. Spread on a paper towel, salt lightly, let sit 5 minutes, pat dry.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Whisk rice vinegar, sesame oil, monk fruit sweetener, and salt until dissolved. Add red pepper flakes if using.

instructions.title: Toss and serve
instructions.body: Add cucumbers and green onions to the dressing, toss to coat. Scatter sesame seeds over the top.

recipes.notes: English cucumbers work best, thinner skin and fewer seeds. Can be made up to an hour ahead.

recipes.name: Kale, Apple, and Goat Cheese Salad with Granola Croutons
recipes.description: Massaged kale, apple, goat cheese, dried cranberries, and a lemon mustard dressing, topped with homemade nut and seed granola. Use certified GF oats. For Meg: skip the granola and cranberries to keep it low glycemic.
recipes.tagline: Massaged kale, crisp apple, and crumbled goat cheese tossed in a tangy lemon dressing, topped with homemade granola croutons.

instructions.title: Make the granola
instructions.body: Preheat oven to 350°F. Combine oats, pecans, pepitas, sunflower seeds, salt, cinnamon, and cayenne. Stir in egg white, oil, and monk fruit sweetener. Spread on a parchment-lined sheet pan, bake 15-17 minutes, stirring once. Cool.

instructions.title: Make the dressing
instructions.body: Combine olive oil, lemon juice, dijon, monk fruit sweetener, and salt. Whisk until blended, season with pepper.

instructions.title: Prepare the kale
instructions.body: Remove ribs, chop leaves small. Sprinkle with a pinch of salt and massage with your hands until darker and fragrant.

instructions.title: Assemble
instructions.body: Drizzle dressing over kale, toss well. Add apple, goat cheese, cranberries, and about half the granola. Toss gently and serve.

recipes.notes: For Meg: skip the granola and dried cranberries to keep this low glycemic. Use certified GF oats. Leftover granola keeps 2 weeks at room temperature.

recipes.name: Roasted Broccoli with Parmesan and Lemon
recipes.description: High-heat roasted broccoli with lemon zest before roasting, lemon juice and parmesan after.
recipes.tagline: Simple roasted broccoli with crispy edges and a bright finish. A reliable side for almost any protein.

instructions.title: Prep and season
instructions.body: Preheat oven to 425°F. Pat broccoli dry, spread on a sheet pan without crowding. Toss with oil, salt, pepper, garlic, and lemon zest.

instructions.title: Roast
instructions.body: Roast 20-22 minutes, flipping once, until edges are deeply browned and crispy.

instructions.title: Finish and serve
instructions.body: Squeeze lemon juice over the top, scatter parmesan, add red pepper flakes if using, toss and serve.

recipes.notes: Don't crowd the pan, the florets will steam instead of roast. Zest goes on before roasting, juice and parmesan go on after.

recipes.name: Blistered Cherry Tomatoes
recipes.description: Hot-pan blistered tomatoes with garlic, olive oil, and fresh basil. Ready in 5 minutes.
recipes.tagline: Blistered cherry tomatoes with garlic and olive oil. A quick, bright side that pairs well with steak and roasted broccoli.

instructions.title: Heat the pan
instructions.body: Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat until shimmering and just starting to smoke.

instructions.title: Blister the tomatoes
instructions.body: Add tomatoes in a single layer, do not move them, cook 2-3 minutes until skins blister and char.

instructions.title: Add garlic
instructions.body: Add garlic and red pepper flakes if using. Toss once, cook 60-90 seconds more until garlic is golden.

instructions.title: Season and serve
instructions.body: Remove from heat, season with salt and pepper, scatter basil over the top, serve immediately.

recipes.notes: A very hot pan is key. Don't move the tomatoes too early or they'll steam instead of char.
